comments.author: @crimsonpride48
comments.text: Well as of 7-10-26 it doesn’t look like that now 😂

replies.author: @Outland_US
replies.text: Thank you for the update. Things change fast. Given the realities of 2026, do you think Russia still holds any appeal for international retirement planning, or is it completely off the table now? What changed the most?

comments.author: @justgoballistic
comments.text: I thought this was going to be current. NOT

comments.author: @AmazingGlobalDiscoveries
comments.text: Can confirm the dirty car rule is real! 😂 Got pulled over once just because a slush storm blacked out my back license plate. You learn to keep wet wipes in the glove box real quick! And I love Moscow 👏

replies.author: @Outland_US
replies.text: Haha, glad you survived the slush storms! It shows the grit needed to live there. Despite the challenges, some find the community vibe perfect for their retirement planning. Would you go back?

comments.author: @NICEGLOBE05
comments.text: An absolute visual masterpiece! The cinematic drone transitions from the grand canals of St. Petersburg to the historic heart of Moscow captured the melancholic yet beautiful soul of Russia perfectly. 10/10 travel journalism!

replies.author: @Outland_US
replies.text: Thank you! With such stunning scenery and an affordable lifestyle, many expats are actually eyeing this region for their retirement planning. Do you think a place this beautiful could be your future home?

comments.author: @MohammadIqbal-mt6ux
comments.text: It would be more interesting video clip if there were some information about Muslim and mosqu and foods. It is very informative anyway thanks much

replies.author: @Outland_US
replies.text: That is a great suggestion! Incorporating local culture and food is key. Since these elements define the quality of life, would they influence your own retirement planning if you moved here?
